summary: Good‑quality soundboard capture of a mixed ’69 show highlighted by Big Boy Pete, Good Lovin', He Was A Friend Of Mine and It’s All Over Now
review: The recording is a solid soundboard source with clear mix on the later tracks (especially Big Boy Pete) but the first three cuts suffer from low volume and tape‑hiss. Musically the set is uneven – some reviewers call it a “walk‑through” while others celebrate extended jams and strong vocal performances, particularly Garcia’s tone and Pigpen’s contributions.

ai_rating.rationale: Average to good show quality (mixed reviews) combined with generally good soundboard recording yields a mid‑range rating; sufficient number of detailed reviews supports confidence.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Low volume on first three tracks, tape hiss present but removable with EQ. Later tracks (Big Boy Pete) have proper levels and a clean mix; overall recording is listenable.

standout_songs: He Was A Friend Of Mine
show_quality.setlist_flow: Unusual ’69 set with a long He Was A Friend Of Mine jam, solid Good Lovin' and the rare cover All Over Now; some feel the band moves sluggishly early on.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Tone described as rock‑solid and strong; leads vocals on Good Lovin'.
